clusters.title: Scientists discover enzyme that could supercharge Ozempic
clusters.summary: Researchers have found an enzyme that can turn fragile drug molecules into durable ring shapes. This could help medications like Ozempic last longer and work more effectively. The process is simpler and more precise than traditional methods, even for complex drugs.

items.title: Scientists discover enzyme that could supercharge Ozempic
items.description: Researchers have found an enzyme that can turn fragile drug molecules into durable ring shapes. This could help medications like Ozempic last longer and work more effectively. The process is simpler and more precise than traditional methods, even for complex drugs. It may open the door to stronger, longer-lasting treatments.

clusters.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
clusters.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to understanding disease risk. It may even lead to future screening tests that detect cancer risk earlier.

items.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
items.description: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to understanding disease risk. It may even lead to future screening tests that detect cancer risk earlier.

clusters.title: This common gout drug may slash heart attack and stroke risk
clusters.summary: A major new study reveals that treating gout may do far more than ease painful joint flare-ups—it could also protect the heart. Researchers found that patients who took common gout medications like allopurinol and successfully lowered their blood urate levels had a significantly reduced risk of heart attacks, strokes, and cardiovascular death over five years.

items.title: This common gout drug may slash heart attack and stroke risk
items.description: A major new study reveals that treating gout may do far more than ease painful joint flare-ups—it could also protect the heart. Researchers found that patients who took common gout medications like allopurinol and successfully lowered their blood urate levels had a significantly reduced risk of heart attacks, strokes, and cardiovascular death over five years.

clusters.title: Scientists say 7 days of meditation can rewire your brain
clusters.summary: A single week of intensive meditation and mind-body practices led to measurable changes across the brain and body. Researchers observed improved brain efficiency, boosted immune signaling, and increased natural pain relief chemicals in participants’ blood. The effects even promoted neuron growth and stronger brain connectivity.

items.title: Scientists say 7 days of meditation can rewire your brain
items.description: A single week of intensive meditation and mind-body practices led to measurable changes across the brain and body. Researchers observed improved brain efficiency, boosted immune signaling, and increased natural pain relief chemicals in participants’ blood. The effects even promoted neuron growth and stronger brain connectivity. Surprisingly, the experience mirrored psychedelic-like brain states—without any drugs involved.

clusters.title: Scientists just found a hidden “drain” inside the human brain
clusters.summary: A hidden waste-removal pathway in the brain has finally been caught in action. Using cutting-edge MRI scans, researchers discovered that fluid flows along the middle meningeal artery in a slow, lymphatic-like pattern—very different from blood. This confirms the presence of a previously unknown drainage hub in humans.

items.title: Scientists just found a hidden “drain” inside the human brain
items.description: A hidden waste-removal pathway in the brain has finally been caught in action. Using cutting-edge MRI scans, researchers discovered that fluid flows along the middle meningeal artery in a slow, lymphatic-like pattern—very different from blood. This confirms the presence of a previously unknown drainage hub in humans. The finding could transform how scientists approach brain aging, injury, and diseases like Alzheimer’s.

clusters.title: Scientists found a protein that drives brain aging — and how to stop it
clusters.summary: Scientists have uncovered a powerful new clue in the mystery of brain aging: a single protein called FTL1. In aging mice, higher levels of this protein weakened connections between brain cells and led to memory decline. But when researchers reduced FTL1, something remarkable happened — the brain began to recover, rebuilding lost connections and restoring memory performance.

items.title: Scientists found a protein that drives brain aging — and how to stop it
items.description: Scientists have uncovered a powerful new clue in the mystery of brain aging: a single protein called FTL1. In aging mice, higher levels of this protein weakened connections between brain cells and led to memory decline. But when researchers reduced FTL1, something remarkable happened — the brain began to recover, rebuilding lost connections and restoring memory performance.

clusters.title: Tabletop games like D&D act as “drama therapy in the wild” to boost players’ self-concepts
clusters.summary: Immersing yourself in a tabletop role-playing game could boost your real-world self-esteem. According to a new Transcultural Psychiatry study, building strong, personal bonds with fictional avatars allows players to safely explore and improve their actual identities.

items.title: Tabletop games like D&D act as “drama therapy in the wild” to boost players’ self-concepts
items.description: Immersing yourself in a tabletop role-playing game could boost your real-world self-esteem. According to a new Transcultural Psychiatry study, building strong, personal bonds with fictional avatars allows players to safely explore and improve their actual identities.

clusters.title: A simple “blank screen” test revealed a key fact about the psychology of neuroticism
clusters.summary: When left with nothing to do, highly neurotic individuals tend to focus on their problems and uncertainties. Recent research suggests this natural tendency to generate negative thoughts explains why they often feel anxious without any immediate stressors.

items.title: A simple “blank screen” test revealed a key fact about the psychology of neuroticism
items.description: When left with nothing to do, highly neurotic individuals tend to focus on their problems and uncertainties. Recent research suggests this natural tendency to generate negative thoughts explains why they often feel anxious without any immediate stressors.

clusters.title: What computer simulations reveal about the evolutionary purpose of gaming
clusters.summary: New research in Evolution and Human Behavior suggests the social benefits of gaming depend on the context. While games might not immediately forge friendships, computer simulations indicate the behavior evolved as a survival strategy to assess potential allies.

items.title: What computer simulations reveal about the evolutionary purpose of gaming
items.description: New research in Evolution and Human Behavior suggests the social benefits of gaming depend on the context. While games might not immediately forge friendships, computer simulations indicate the behavior evolved as a survival strategy to assess potential allies.

clusters.title: Trump’s 2024 victory flipped the psychological differences between liberals and conservatives
clusters.summary: A newly published study challenges the idea that liberals and conservatives have inherently different worldviews, revealing that traits like cynicism and institutional trust flip dramatically depending on whose political party holds the White House.

items.title: Trump’s 2024 victory flipped the psychological differences between liberals and conservatives
items.description: A newly published study challenges the idea that liberals and conservatives have inherently different worldviews, revealing that traits like cynicism and institutional trust flip dramatically depending on whose political party holds the White House.

clusters.title: These overlooked brain cells may control fear and PTSD
clusters.summary: Astrocytes, once thought to be mere brain “support cells,” are now revealed to be key players in fear memory. Researchers found they actively help form, recall, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. Changing astrocyte activity directly altered how strong fear memories became.

items.title: These overlooked brain cells may control fear and PTSD
items.description: Astrocytes, once thought to be mere brain “support cells,” are now revealed to be key players in fear memory. Researchers found they actively help form, recall, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. Changing astrocyte activity directly altered how strong fear memories became. This breakthrough could lead to entirely new treatments for anxiety-related disorders.

clusters.title: Your brain could help solve autism and most people don’t know it
clusters.summary: A new survey reveals a striking disconnect in how Americans think about autism research. While nearly everyone agrees that studying the autistic brain is essential, most people are unaware that brain donation after death is a key part of making that research possible. Unlike organ donation, brain donation is a separate process, and widespread confusion remains about how it works, when it must occur, and who can participate.

items.title: Your brain could help solve autism and most people don’t know it
items.description: A new survey reveals a striking disconnect in how Americans think about autism research. While nearly everyone agrees that studying the autistic brain is essential, most people are unaware that brain donation after death is a key part of making that research possible. Unlike organ donation, brain donation is a separate process, and widespread confusion remains about how it works, when it must occur, and who can participate.

clusters.title: Huge study finds no evidence cannabis helps anxiety, depression, or PTSD
clusters.summary: The largest review of medicinal cannabis to date found it doesn’t effectively treat anxiety, depression, or PTSD—despite millions using it for those reasons. Researchers warn it could even make mental health worse, raising risks like psychosis and addiction while delaying proven treatments. Some limited benefits were seen for conditions like insomnia and autism, but the evidence is weak.

items.title: Huge study finds no evidence cannabis helps anxiety, depression, or PTSD
items.description: The largest review of medicinal cannabis to date found it doesn’t effectively treat anxiety, depression, or PTSD—despite millions using it for those reasons. Researchers warn it could even make mental health worse, raising risks like psychosis and addiction while delaying proven treatments. Some limited benefits were seen for conditions like insomnia and autism, but the evidence is weak. The findings are fueling calls for stricter oversight as cannabis use continues to rise.

clusters.title: Fathers face rising depression risk a year after baby arrives
clusters.summary: New fathers appear to have fewer mental health diagnoses during pregnancy and the early months after birth. But that early stability does not last. About a year later, depression and stress-related disorders increase significantly, surprising researchers.

items.title: Fathers face rising depression risk a year after baby arrives
items.description: New fathers appear to have fewer mental health diagnoses during pregnancy and the early months after birth. But that early stability does not last. About a year later, depression and stress-related disorders increase significantly, surprising researchers. The findings suggest that the emotional toll of fatherhood builds over time rather than hitting immediately.

clusters.title: How the immune system battles lifelong viral infections acquired at birth
clusters.summary: Millions of people worldwide carry viral infections they acquired at birth, often for life. For a long time it was assumed that the immune system hardly fights these pathogens. Researchers from the University of Basel show now that the body's defenses do indeed act against the virus.

items.title: How the immune system battles lifelong viral infections acquired at birth
items.description: Millions of people worldwide carry viral infections they acquired at birth, often for life. For a long time it was assumed that the immune system hardly fights these pathogens. Researchers from the University of Basel show now that the body's defenses do indeed act against the virus. This could be a useful starting point for future therapies.

clusters.title: Independent, academic cancer trials are vital to improve patient outcomes worldwide
clusters.summary: Independent, academic clinical cancer trials are essential to improving patient outcomes, reducing inequalities in care, and strengthening health care systems worldwide, according to a new initiative published in The Lancet Oncology. The paper, "Academic clinical cancer trials to improve patient outcomes," announces the launch of a Lancet Oncology Commission examining the role, relevance, and challenges of academic clinical cancer research in today's geopolitical and economic context.

items.title: Independent, academic cancer trials are vital to improve patient outcomes worldwide
items.description: Independent, academic clinical cancer trials are essential to improving patient outcomes, reducing inequalities in care, and strengthening health care systems worldwide, according to a new initiative published in The Lancet Oncology. The paper, "Academic clinical cancer trials to improve patient outcomes," announces the launch of a Lancet Oncology Commission examining the role, relevance, and challenges of academic clinical cancer research in today's geopolitical and economic context.

clusters.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
clusters.summary: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits.

items.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
items.description: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality olive oil could be a simple way to support brain health as you age.

clusters.title: 95% of people carry this virus and scientists may have just found how to stop it
clusters.summary: Scientists have taken a major step toward stopping Epstein-Barr virus (EBV), an extremely common infection linked to cancer and chronic disease. By using mice engineered with human antibody genes, researchers created powerful human-like antibodies that block the virus from attaching to and entering immune cells. One of these antibodies completely prevented infection in lab models with human immune systems, marking a breakthrough after years of difficulty tackling EBV’s ability to invade nearly all B cells.

clusters.title: Deterioration in cognitive function precedes cardiovascular disease events in seniors
clusters.summary: For older adults, deterioration in cognitive function is seen prior to cardiovascular disease (CVD) events, according to a study published online April 20 in JAMA Network Open.

items.title: Deterioration in cognitive function precedes cardiovascular disease events in seniors
items.description: For older adults, deterioration in cognitive function is seen prior to cardiovascular disease (CVD) events, according to a study published online April 20 in JAMA Network Open.

clusters.title: Can neuroscience shed light on pregnancy complications?
clusters.summary: Pregnancy risks suddenly spike after age 35, something doctors generally attribute to problems with the fertilized egg. But Wu Tsai Neurosciences Institute postdoctoral scholar Blake Laham is not convinced that's the whole story. For one thing, why does risk increase dramatically at an age when most other aspects of women's health remain stable?
